archetype: Automated Ocean Fertilization Monitoring and Carbon Sequestration Tracking System for Phytoplankton-Based Climate Mitigation
domain: Ocean Fertilization and Climate Change Mitigation
description: This system monitors and tracks the effects of ocean fertilization on phytoplankton growth, carbon sequestration, and ocean chemistry, providing insights for climate change mitigation strategies. It is used by oceanographers, climate scientists, and policymakers to inform large-scale ocean fertilization efforts. The system must handle complex data from various sources, including satellite imagery, autonomous underwater vehicles, and sensor arrays.
rules: MUST integrate with NASA's Ocean Color Web service to retrieve satellite-derived phytoplankton biomass data
MUST comply with the International Maritime Organization's (IMO) regulations on ocean fertilization
MUST support data ingestion from autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) and sensor arrays
MUST implement data quality control and validation procedures to ensure accuracy of carbon sequestration estimates
MUST provide real-time alerts and notifications for changes in ocean chemistry and phytoplankton growth
anti_patterns: DO NOT assume uniform phytoplankton growth patterns across different ocean regions
DO NOT neglect to account for ocean currents and mixing in carbon sequestration estimates
DO NOT use simplistic models that fail to capture complex interactions between phytoplankton, nutrients, and ocean chemistry
key_technical_challenges: Integrating diverse data sources with varying spatial and temporal resolutions
Developing accurate models of phytoplankton growth and carbon sequestration under different ocean conditions
Ensuring data quality and validation procedures to support reliable climate change mitigation strategies

recommended_stack.language: Python + extensive libraries for data analysis and scientific computing
recommended_stack.framework: Flask + support for real-time data ingestion and API-based data exchange
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL + support for spatial data types and queries
recommended_stack.cache: Redis + fast data retrieval and caching for real-time applications
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka + support for high-throughput data ingestion and processing

compliance_flags: NASA Data Sharing and Access Policy
